title: ageratina cartagoensis
intro: Ageratina cartagoensis, also known as Cartago Mountain Snakeroot, is a plant species belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae) that is native to Costa Rica. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to treat various health conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The plant is rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its health benefits, including its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Ageratina cartagoensis can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health issues, making it a valuable herbal remedy.
